title: Vue Computed Properties Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of dependency tracking, caching, and Computed vs. Methods.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary benefit of a Computed Property in Vue.js?
options: They are cached and only re-evaluate when their dependencies change.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which option is used to define Computed Properties in a Vue instance?
What happens when a dependency of a Computed Property changes?
options: Vue automatically re-evaluates the Computed Property.

questions.question: What is the key difference between a Computed Property and a Method?
options: Computed Properties are cached, while Methods are not (they always run again).
Methods are only for class components.

questions.question: Computed Properties should generally be used for data formatting and calculations, not for side effects like API calls.
options: False (they are perfect for API calls)
